prs.title: fix #87700: [Bug]: Control UI webchat session silently resets after network disconnect / sleep — previous session file renamed to .jsonl.reset.* and lost from sessions tab
prs.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Control UI can keep showing a conversation after gateway reconnect, but the next send can roll the stale transcript into `.jsonl.reset.*` and make it disappear from the sessions tab.
- Solution: pass the client-selected `sessionId` through `chat.send`, but only allow it to suppress idle/daily rollover when Control UI also sends the one-shot reconnect resume signal.
- What changed: `chat.send` now accepts `resumeSession`, Control UI sets it after a gateway disconnect for the current visible session, and `initSessionState` preserves configured idle/daily rollover for ordinary sends.
- What did NOT change: explicit `/new`, mismatched session ids, and ordinary stale Control UI sends without the reconnect resume signal still rotate normally.
- Fixes #87700

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** after a sleep/disconnect cycle, Control UI can still hold the visible session id. A reconnect resume send should keep that session instead of rolling the transcript to `.reset.*`, while ordinary stale sends still follow the configured reset policy.
- **Real environment tested:** live local OpenClaw dev gateway started from this PR worktree on Linux, using an isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`/state dir and a real WebSocket `connect` + `chat.send` request. The proof pre-seeded a stale `sessions.json` entry and transcript file, paired a temporary signed webchat device, sent `chat.send` with `sessionId` and `resumeSession: true` through the running gateway, then inspected the actual session store and sessions directory.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```sh
  OPENCLAW_REPO="$TASK_WORKTREE" start_openclaw_dev_gateway.sh
  node --import tsx "$EVIDENCE_DIR/live-gateway-session-proof.mjs"
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:** terminal console output from the live gateway proof:
  ```text
  gatewayConnect= ok
  chatSend= ok
  resumeSession= true
  storeSessionBefore= sess-live-reconnect-proof
  storeSessionAfter= sess-live-reconnect-proof
  resetFiles= 0
  proof= live gateway chat.send resumeSession preserved the asserted webchat session without .reset rollover
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** the running gateway accepted the real webchat `chat.send`, kept the `agent:main:main` store entry on the original session id, and produced zero `.reset.*` transcript files. Focused regression tests also prove the same asserted `sessionId` does not bypass idle/daily rollover unless the reconnect resume signal is present.
- **What was not tested:** I did not reproduce a physical Mac sleep/Docker/Chrome wake cycle. The live proof covers the same gateway/WebSocket send path and session-store rollover decision in a local isolated gateway process; the Control UI unit tests cover the one-shot reconnect marker.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts`, `ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts`, `ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts`, and `packages/gateway-protocol/src/index.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** gateway `chat.send` forwards `sessionId` and `resumeSession`, Control UI emits `resumeSession` once after reconnect, reconnect resume preserves the visible session, ordinary stale sends with only `sessionId` still rotate, and stale/mismatched asserted ids still reset.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** the issue is caused by the handoff between Control UI reconnect state, the gateway `chat.send` request, and the session reset owner, so tests cover each boundary plus the stale-rollover compatibility case.
- **Commands run:**
  ```sh
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts packages/gateway-protocol/src/index.test.ts
  git diff --check
  ```
- **Test output:** unit shard passed 39 tests; gateway shard passed 30 tests; auto-reply shard passed 99 tests; UI shard passed 62 tests; unit-UI shard passed 92 tests. `git diff --check` passed.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** `chat.send` parsed the client-supplied `sessionId`, but reply initialization did not receive a reconnect-specific resume intent. Passing `sessionId` alone was not enough because Control UI also sends the current session id on ordinary follow-up sends, so treating any matching id as fresh would disable configured idle/daily rollover.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** Control UI now records the visible session id when the gateway disconnects and sends `resumeSession` once when the next send targets that same session. `initSessionState` only treats the matching requested id as reusable when that reconnect resume signal is present; otherwise the existing reset freshness policy remains the source of truth.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High
- **Patch quality notes:** The mock `return undefined` path in the gateway test only exposes captured reply options without running a model call. The `as unknown` casts are existing UI test/helper boundary patterns for exercising controller functions with partial host objects; they do not suppress type errors or change runtime behavior. The live gateway proof exercises the running WebSocket/RPC path and session store, and the session regression tests cover the ordinary stale rollover compatibility case.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The session store and existing reset policy remain the source of truth. This patch adds one additive `chat.send` protocol hint and does not add a new session store, config surface, or reset policy bypass for ordinary sends.

prs.title: Replay sessions_yield wait text in WebChat history
prs.body:
Fixes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87321

## User-facing bug
WebChat replay could drop a visible `sessions_yield` wait message when the message only existed in the transcript as a `toolResult` payload. The raw `toolResult` role is not a stable display surface for WebChat history, so reopening the session could hide text such as "Waiting for child completion."

## Exact repro
1. Create or load a session transcript containing an assistant `sessions_yield` tool call followed by a successful `toolResult`:
   - `toolName: "sessions_yield"`
   - `content` or `details` containing `{ "status": "yielded", "message": "Waiting for child completion." }`
2. Request `chat.history` / WebChat replay for that session.
3. Before this fix, the user-visible yield text could be absent because the display path left it as a raw `toolResult`.
4. After this fix, replay includes a normal assistant text message with the yielded wait text and avoids duplicating that text in live single-message or incremental SSE paths.

## Fix summary
- Added a `sessions_yield` display projection in `src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts`.
- Successful `sessions_yield` tool results now become assistant-shaped text mirrors marked with `openclawSessionsYieldMirror`.
- The raw successful `sessions_yield` `toolResult` is removed from displayed history so WebChat does not depend on rendering tool-result JSON.
- The mirror keeps transcript metadata from the tool result so pagination and SSE history cursors continue to point at the original transcript sequence.
- Single-message live projection does not mirror `sessions_yield` results, preventing duplicate live chat output.
- Incremental `SessionHistorySseState` appends dedupe explicit `sessions_yield` results against the previous visible assistant wait text.
- Reused existing `sessions_yield` result detection from `subagent-yield-output` to stay aligned with subagent wait-state semantics.

## Targeted tests
- Added `SessionHistorySseState` coverage proving `buildSessionHistorySnapshot` projects the yield message, removes the raw `toolResult`, marks the mirror, and preserves the transcript seq.
- Added `SessionHistorySseState` coverage proving single-message projection does not mirror `sessions_yield` without prior assistant context.
- Added `SessionHistorySseState` coverage proving incremental SSE appends do not duplicate an assistant wait message when the later `sessions_yield` result arrives.
- Added gateway `chat.history` coverage for a transcript-backed `sessions_yield` result replay.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: WebChat replay now turns successful transcript-backed `sessions_yield` tool results into visible assistant wait text, instead of relying on raw `toolResult` rendering.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/webchat-replay-sessions-yield`, commit `ed759af5`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts src/gateway/session-history-state.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --run --maxWorkers 1 src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts src/gateway/session-history-state.test.ts -t sessions_yield`
- `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --run --maxWorkers 1 src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts src/gateway/session-history-state.test.ts`
- `node --import tsx -e "<buildSessionHistorySnapshot interleaved sessions_yield transcript proof>"`
- `codex review --base origin/main`

Evidence after fix:
- Focused Vitest passed: `Test Files 4 passed (4)`, `Tests 10 passed | 83 skipped (93)`.
- Broader replay/history Vitest passed: `Test Files 4 passed (4)`, `Tests 93 passed (93)`.
- Direct local replay projection proof for the review-requested interleaved shape passed with `node --import tsx`; copied terminal output:

```json
[
  { "role": "user", "text": "spawn a child", "seq": 1 },
  { "role": "assistant", "seq": 2 },
  { "role": "assistant", "text": "I started the child session.", "seq": 3 },
  {
    "role": "assistant",
    "text": "Waiting for child completion.",
    "mirror": { "toolName": "sessions_yield", "toolCallId": "call-yield" },
    "seq": 4
  }
]
```

- `git diff --check` passed.
- `codex review --base origin/main` passed after resolving duplicate live projection and incremental SSE findings.

Observed result after fix: History snapshots include an assistant-shaped yield mirror with the wait text and transcript seq metadata; raw successful `sessions_yield` tool results are removed from display history, interleaved visible assistant text between the call and result no longer hides the yield mirror, and live/incremental paths avoid duplicate output.

What was not tested: `pnpm check:changed` did not reach changed-file checks because dependency installation hit npm DNS failures (`ENOTFOUND`) in the sandbox. The new focused Vitest target for the interleaved `sessions_yield` regression stalled with no output in this worktree and was terminated by timeout; the direct `node --import tsx` projection proof above exercised the same `buildSessionHistorySnapshot` path.

## Not tested / known gaps
- No browser session replay was manually driven through the Control UI; coverage is through Gateway history and session history state tests.

## AI-assisted disclosure
This patch was prepared with AI assistance. I used local code inspection, targeted regression tests, and explicit command output to validate the behavior above.

prs.title: fix: resolve QMD Windows shims and guard image URL downloads
prs.body:
## Summary

Fixes the Windows QMD shim path and narrows the image URL work into a guarded provider-download path.

- Resolve Windows `.cmd` shims that point at quoted absolute JavaScript entrypoints.
- Use the real QMD package name, `@tobilu/qmd`, for package-bin fallback resolution.
- Parse OpenAI-compatible image responses that return `data[].url` instead of `data[].b64_json`.
- Route provider-returned image URLs through guarded HTTP with bounded reads, MIME validation, scoped same-origin transport reuse, configured media limits, and operation-deadline timeouts.
- Dropped the unrelated `node-llama-cpp` dependency/lockfile churn from the original PR scope.

## Behavior addressed

Windows QMD availability checks no longer fail when `qmd.cmd` wraps a quoted absolute Node entrypoint instead of a `%~dp0`-relative entrypoint. OpenAI-compatible URL-backed image generation responses now produce image assets without using raw fetch or broad private-network carryover.

## Real environment tested

- AWS Crabbox native Windows: `cbx_36917d25d801`, run `run_0dd48bf72083`, hydrate workflow `26722175678`.
- AWS Crabbox Windows WSL2: `cbx_92cdb109d982`, run `run_740f8e92e641`, hydrate workflow `26721781232`.
- AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate after rebase: `cbx_0b074a7498e9`, run `run_72f46782c53c`.

## Exact steps or command run after this patch

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/windows-spawn.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/qmd-process.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts src/image-generation/image-assets.test.ts src/image-generation/openai-compatible-image-provider.test.ts extensions/openai/image-generation-provider.test.ts
node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check
node scripts/docs-list.js
node scripts/check-plugin-sdk-subpath-exports.mjs
git diff --check
node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --target linux --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"
```

Native Windows and WSL2 ran the same focused Vitest set on hydrated Crabbox workspaces. The final Linux changed gate was run after rebasing onto current `origin/main`.

## Evidence after fix

- Local post-rebase focused tests: 6 shards passed, 218 tests total.
- Native Windows Crabbox focused tests: 6 shards passed, 213 tests total on `run_0dd48bf72083`.
- WSL2 Crabbox focused tests: 6 shards passed, 215 tests total on `run_740f8e92e641`.
- Plugin SDK API baseline check passed after regenerating the baseline across the final rebase.
- SDK subpath export check passed.
- Autoreview passed clean after the URL-download security/timeout/boundary fixes.

## Observed result after fix

- Quoted absolute Windows `.cmd` QMD shims resolve to direct Node execution without shell fallback.
- Scoped QMD package fallback resolves through `@tobilu/qmd`.
- URL-backed OpenAI-compatible image responses are accepted and converted into generated image assets.
- Cross-origin provider-returned URLs do not inherit provider private-network/dispatcher/auth policy.
- Same-origin provider-returned URLs keep provider auth/transport policy and still use guarded fetch.
- URL downloads honor configured generated-media byte limits and share the image operation deadline instead of resetting timeout per download.

## What was not tested

Full live QMD memory indexing/search was not run. The final `pnpm check:changed` gate is blocked by a current `origin/main` type error in untouched `src/channels/message/ingress-queue.test.ts` from `b0679d1f13d`; this PR does not touch channel ingress queue files.

prs.title: feat(hooks): add agent:turn:end and agent:turn:save internal hook events
prs.body:
## Summary

- Adds two ACP turn internal hook events:
  - `agent:turn:end` for ACP runtime turn completion or failure.
  - `agent:turn:save` for the save-layer outcome after transcript persistence is saved, skipped, or fails.
- Wires dispatch-backed ACP turns and direct ACP command turns through the awaited save callback before reporting the save outcome.
- Adds `context.saveOutcome` (`saved`, `skipped`, `failed`) and optional `context.saveSkipReason`; `context.success` is true only when `saveOutcome === "saved"`.
- Requires explicit save evidence before reporting `saved`; missing callbacks and no-op callbacks report skipped outcomes instead.
- Validates structured save callback results and reports malformed results as failed save outcomes.
- Tracks actual transcript append evidence so blocked/no-op writes report `skipped` instead of `saved`.
- Documents the split contract and covers manager, dispatch, direct command, persistence, and type-guard paths with focused tests.

## Change Type

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #37833
- Related #60734

## Behavior Changes

Internal hook consumers can subscribe to two ACP turn boundaries:

| Event | Fires when | Durable turn data readable |
|---|---|---|
| `agent:turn:end` | ACP runtime turn completes or fails after terminal event delivery | No |
| `agent:turn:save` | ACP save phase reaches an outcome | Yes, when `context.saveOutcome === "saved"` |

`agent:turn:end` carries `sessionKey`, `success`, `durationMs`, and optional `errorCode`.

`agent:turn:save` carries `sessionKey`, `success`, `saveOutcome`, `turnSuccess`, `durationMs`, optional `turnErrorCode`, optional `saveError`, and optional `saveSkipReason`.

No user-facing messaging or config behavior changes.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

## Verification

Commands run on head `e1f18ce5ee`:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts src/hooks/internal-hooks.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/commands/agent.acp.test.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/agents/agent-command.live-model-switch.test.ts
pnpm check:test-types
pnpm exec oxlint src/acp/control-plane/manager.core.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.types.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts src/hooks/internal-hooks.ts src/hooks/internal-hooks.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/agents/agent-command.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-transcript.runtime.ts src/commands/agent.acp.test.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/agents/agent-command.live-model-switch.test.ts
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/automation/hooks.md src/acp/control-plane/manager.core.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.types.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts src/hooks/internal-hooks.ts src/hooks/internal-hooks.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/agents/agent-command.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-transcript.runtime.ts src/commands/agent.acp.test.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/agents/agent-command.live-model-switch.test.ts
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
git diff --check
```

Result:

```text
5 Vitest shards passed: manager, hooks, ACP transcript persistence, direct ACP command, dispatch ACP, dispatch-from-config, ACP live-model-switch command
Test type check passed
Touched-file oxlint passed
Touched-file formatting check passed
git diff --check passed
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: ACP save readiness is now reported only after the awaited save callback returns an explicit validated outcome. Direct ACP and dispatch ACP transcript persistence run inside that callback, so `saveOutcome: "saved"` means the application-level transcript append path completed or confirmed persisted content; `skipped` and `failed` are reported separately. Missing save callbacks and no-op save callbacks report skipped outcomes.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout on PR head `e1f18ce5ee`, macOS, Node `v25.6.0`, pnpm `11.2.2`.
- **Exact steps or command run after the patch**: Ran a local `pnpm exec tsx --eval` command importing production `persistAcpTurnTranscript`, writing a prompt/reply pair through the ACP transcript persistence helper into a temporary session store, then reading the resulting transcript file.
- **Evidence after fix**: Redacted terminal capture from that command:

```json
{
  "saveOutcome": "saved",
  "saveSkipReason": null,
  "hasSessionFile": true,
  "transcriptHasPrompt": true,
  "transcriptHasReply": true,
  "transcriptLineCount": 7
}
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The ACP transcript persistence helper returned `saveOutcome: "saved"` only after producing a session file whose transcript contained both the prompt and reply text. Separate focused tests cover `no_save_callback`, `no_save_evidence`, malformed callback results, blocked message writes, dispatch ACP, and direct ACP command callback wiring.
- **What was not tested**: A networked external `acpx` adapter process.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Failure Recovery

Revert this PR to remove the new internal hook events and return ACP turn completion to stats-only behavior.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Consumers may confuse runtime completion with durable transcript readiness.
- Mitigation: Separate `agent:turn:end` from `agent:turn:save`, with `saveOutcome` identifying `saved`, `skipped`, or `failed`.
- Risk: Slow transcript persistence could delay ACP cleanup.
- Mitigation: Only the save callback is awaited for the save boundary; hook handlers still use bounded fire-and-forget execution.

prs.title: Emit sessions.changed for in-chat command metadata
prs.body:
Fixes #88598

## User-facing bug

Control UI clients subscribed to session updates could miss metadata changes made by in-chat commands. A command-only turn such as `/goal complete`, `/goal pause`, or `/goal clear` can update the session store without starting a model run, but the Gateway did not emit a `sessions.changed` event for that mutation. The visible session row could keep stale goal/status metadata until a manual reload or another unrelated session event.

## Exact repro

Source-level reproduction:

1. Connect a Control UI/WebChat client that subscribes to `sessions.changed`.
2. Send an in-chat `/goal` command that mutates the persisted session goal, for example `/goal complete shipped`.
3. The command handler writes the updated goal to the session store and returns command output.
4. Before this patch, no command-metadata `sessions.changed` event was broadcast for the store write, so subscribed clients did not get a scoped refresh for that session.

## Fix summary

- Add a typed `onSessionMetadataChanged` reply option for command handlers.
- Notify after successful command-session store writes through the shared `persistSessionEntry` helper.
- Notify `/goal` start, pause, resume, complete, blocked, clear, and status paths when they actually change the persisted goal metadata.
- Share the Gateway `sessions.changed` emitter between session RPC handlers and `chat.send`, so command-only chat turns can broadcast a scoped session row update.
- Delay command-metadata broadcasts until after the current command turn leaves the active-run map, while still preserving `hasActiveRun: true` if another run for the same session is active.
- Fall back to the shared session lifecycle event bus when command metadata changes happen outside `chat.send`, so raw channel dispatchers that use the same command-session store helper also refresh Gateway subscribers.
- Preserve selected-agent global alias handling so canonical `global` updates still refresh a selected `agent:<id>:global` Control UI/WebChat session.
- Include live session setting metadata (`thinkingLevels`, `thinkingOptions`, `thinkingDefault`) on command-metadata `sessions.changed` events, and reuse the TUI-loaded model catalog when rebuilding embedded session defaults.
- Document that command-only session metadata changes refresh subscribed clients.

## Targeted tests

- Added `/goal` command unit coverage for metadata-change notifications on mutating goal actions and no-op clear paths.
- Added `/goal` command unit coverage for the shared lifecycle fallback when no direct Gateway subscriber callback is installed.
- Added Gateway sessions coverage for command-metadata `sessions.changed` payloads and active-run exclusion.
- Added Gateway chat coverage proving command-only `/goal` turns broadcast scoped session updates and do not report their own command turn as active.
- Added Gateway lifecycle coverage proving command metadata lifecycle events are broadcast to session subscribers.
- Added UI controller coverage for canonical `global` deltas against selected agent-global aliases.
- Added embedded TUI coverage proving history model catalogs are reused for session metadata/defaults.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: In-chat command metadata writes now emit scoped `sessions.changed` events, so subscribed Control UI/WebChat clients can refresh visible session metadata after command-only turns.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/sessions-changed-in-chat-commands`, commit `ab4c78c7`, rebased onto `origin/main` at `be127318`. The Gateway proof starts a local WebSocket server/client pair and exercises the same `sessions.subscribe` plus `chat.send` event path used by Control UI/WebChat clients. The shared lifecycle fallback proof exercises command metadata notifications when no direct `chat.send` callback is unavailable.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts src/tui/embedded-backend.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.list-changed.test.ts -t "canonical global|history model catalog|live session setting metadata"`
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.sessions.list-changed.test.ts -t "live session setting metadata"`
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/commands-goal.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.model.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.mixed-inline.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-stop-target.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-session-store.test.ts -t "metadata|mixed inline directives|directive-only handling mutates|fast-lane metadata|global-session|directive persistence mutates|passes session metadata|goal|command session metadata"`
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.list-changed.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts -t "broadcasts command metadata lifecycle changes|chat.send broadcasts sessions.changed when in-chat command metadata changes|sessions.changed command metadata excludes only the command run from active state|sessions.changed command metadata marks selected global runs active|sessions.changed mutation events include live session setting metadata"`
- `git diff --check origin/main`
- `codex review --base origin/main`

Evidence after fix:
- UI/TUI metadata proof passed: `Test Files 2 passed (2)`, `Tests 5 passed` for canonical global alias adoption and embedded TUI model catalog reuse. The combined command also attempted the Gateway metadata target, but default sandbox networking returned `listen EPERM 127.0.0.1`; I reran the Gateway target with elevated local-network permission.
- Gateway live setting metadata proof passed with elevated local-network permission: `Test Files 1 passed (1)`, `Tests 1 passed | 14 skipped (15)`.
- Auto-reply command metadata proof passed after the latest rebase: `Test Files 5 passed (5)`, `Tests 29 passed | 70 skipped (99)`. It verifies mutating goal/directive commands call `onSessionMetadataChanged({ sessionKey, reason: "command-metadata" })`, no-op clear paths do not notify, and the shared lifecycle fallback emits `reason: "command-metadata"` when no direct subscriber callback is installed.
- Gateway WebSocket/Control UI proof passed after the latest rebase: the targeted Gateway command passed with `Test Files 5 passed (5)`, `Tests 7 passed | 92 skipped (99)`. It subscribes with `sessions.subscribe`, sends `/goal clear` through `chat.send`, observes a `sessions.changed` event with `reason: "command-metadata"`, verifies command metadata excludes only the command run while preserving active selected global runs, and verifies command metadata mutation events include live session setting metadata.
- Copied Gateway event output from the local WebSocket proof:

```json
{
  "type": "sessions.changed",
  "reason": "command-metadata",
  "sessionKey": "main",
  "hasActiveRun": false,
  "metadata": {
    "thinkingDefault": "medium",
    "thinkingLevels": ["low", "medium", "high"]
  }
}
```

- Shared sessions emitter proof passed in the same Gateway run. It verifies command metadata events exclude the command run itself while preserving `hasActiveRun: true` when another run for the same session is still active.
- Shared lifecycle fallback proof passed in the same Gateway run. It emits `emitSessionLifecycleEvent({ sessionKey, reason: "command-metadata", agentId })` and verifies subscribed clients receive the scoped `sessions.changed` payload with the refreshed session row.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- `codex review --base origin/main` reported no discrete regressions in the command metadata notification paths. Its own Gateway WebSocket test attempt was blocked by sandbox `127.0.0.1` listen `EPERM`, not by a code failure; the elevated local-network Gateway proof above passed.

Observed result after fix: Command-only `/goal` metadata mutations produce scoped `sessions.changed` broadcasts with fresh session row metadata. `chat.send` keeps the command-only active-run exclusion, selected agent-global aliases receive canonical global deltas, and non-`chat.send` command paths get the shared lifecycle fallback instead of leaving subscribers stale.

What was not tested: Full core typecheck is still blocked by pre-existing `ui/src/ui/views/workboard-3d-game.ts` errors for missing `three` type resolution and an implicit `any`. I did not run a live browser Control UI session against a real long-running `/goal` command. The Gateway WebSocket tests require local `127.0.0.1` listening; the default sandbox returned `listen EPERM`, so I reran the targeted Gateway proof with elevated local-network permission. I did not implement or test `/name` because this checkout does not contain a `/name` handler.

## Not tested / known gaps

- This patch focuses on command metadata changes that happen through `chat.send` reply options and the shared command-session store helper.
- Channel command flows that bypass the shared command-session store helper are unchanged.

## AI-assisted disclosure

This patch was prepared with AI assistance. I used source-level reproduction, targeted regression tests, and `codex review --base origin/main` to validate the behavior above.

prs.title: fix(agents): block message-tool spam loops defeated by volatile message ids
prs.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: Issue #89090 reports that `loopDetection` detects repeated `message.send` calls (`ping_pong`, warning level) but can never escalate to a **block**. A model stuck in a tool-call loop keeps sending duplicate messages — 30+ duplicates reported — until `runRetries.max` (default 160) is exhausted; the user only sees a flood of identical messages and the warning the model ignores.
- **Root Cause**: In `tool-loop-detection.ts`, `hashToolOutcome` hashes the full result (`details` + `text`) for the `message` tool. Every successful send returns a fresh `messageId`, so the `resultHash` differs on every call. All three critical-block paths — the global circuit breaker, `ping_pong` critical, and generic no-progress — require consecutive **identical** `resultHash` values (`getNoProgressStreak` / `noProgressEvidence`), which is structurally impossible for a tool whose result carries a unique id. Detection fires only at warning level because it also keys on `argsHash`, which is stable.
- **Fix**: Add a stable-hash case for the `message` tool that drops volatile delivery-id keys before hashing, preserving the stable success / target / channel / text facts.
  - `hashSendToolOutcome(details, text)` omits `VOLATILE_SEND_RESULT_KEYS` (`messageId`, `message_id`, `messageIds`, `id`, `receipt`, `ts`, `timestamp`) from `details` and then digests the rest with `text`, mirroring the existing per-tool stable-hash cases for `exec` and `process` poll/log.
  - It runs at the shared `hashToolOutcome` layer, so it covers direct, gateway, and plugin-handled message results uniformly.
  - Repeated identical-args sends now produce a stable `resultHash`, so the no-progress / critical paths fire and the loop is blocked.
  - **Why this is safe (no false positives)**: no-progress requires BOTH `argsHash` and `resultHash` to repeat. The args hash already carries the message content and target, so two genuinely different messages have different `argsHash` and are never flagged — dropping the volatile id only restores detection for true same-args resends.
- **What changed**:
  - `src/agents/tool-loop-detection.ts` — add `hashSendToolOutcome` + `VOLATILE_SEND_RESULT_KEYS` and branch the `message` tool to it inside `hashToolOutcome`.
  - `src/agents/tool-loop-detection.test.ts` — three regression cases (stable hash across volatile ids, escalation to critical, no false-positive on distinct messages).
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - Generic / exec / process / poll detector semantics, thresholds, and retry caps are untouched — nothing was loosened or raised; this restores the existing block behavior for one tool.
  - No config surface (no schema, defaults, doctor migrations, or `docs/reference/config`).
  - No plugin surface (no plugin SDK, manifest, `extensions/*` api/runtime-api, registry/loader).
  - Gateway protocol unchanged.

### Reproduction

1. Configure an agent with a model prone to tool-call loops on a channel that returns a per-send message id (e.g. Feishu).
2. The model loops on `message.send` with identical arguments.
3. **Before this PR**: `ping_pong` fires at warning level but never reaches critical/block; the user receives 30+ duplicate messages until run-retries are exhausted.
4. **After this PR**: the repeated identical send is recognized as no-progress and escalates to a critical block.

Deterministic source reproduction (added as a regression test): record repeated `message` sends with identical args but a changing `messageId` in the result, then call `detectToolCallLoop` — before the fix it can never reach a critical branch.

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed (#89090 — message-tool loops can now be blocked, not just warned)**: repeated identical `message.send` escalates to a critical block instead of spamming until run-retry exhaustion.

**Real environment tested (Linux, Node — Vitest against the production loop-detection module; no live channel run)**: verified via `tool-loop-detection.test.ts`, plus core production type-check and a scoped test type-check over the two changed files, plus format check. A live multi-channel gateway loop was not run.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch (repo-root)**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tool-loop-detection.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json` (core types) and a scoped `tsgo` over the changed production + test files (test types); `oxfmt --check` on the changed files.

**Evidence after fix (Vitest output for the touched test file)**:

```text
 tool-loop-detection.test.ts   Tests  46 passed (46)
```

Three new cases pass: a stable result hash across two sends whose `messageId` differs; escalation to `critical` after identical-args sends despite every `messageId` differing; and no `stuck` result when the sends carry distinct content (different args).

**Observed result after fix (identical-args send loops reach critical/block)**: `detectToolCallLoop` reports `stuck` at `critical` for repeated identical sends, while distinct-content sends remain unflagged.

**What was not tested (live channel/gateway runaway loop)**: a real Feishu/gateway loop session was not reproduced live; the proof is the deterministic module-level reproduction the issue itself describes.

**Repro confirmation**: with the production `message` branch removed on an otherwise-identical tree, the two behavior cases fail (stable-hash and escalation-to-critical), and they pass with the fix — so the tests cover the production change rather than passing vacuously.

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: over-stripping result fields could collide distinct outcomes. **Mitigation**: only a small set of volatile delivery-id keys is removed; stable facts (status, target/chatId, action, text) and the `argsHash` gate remain, so distinct messages stay distinguishable (covered by the no-false-positive regression test).
- **Risk**: behavior change could surface previously-hidden blocks. **Mitigation**: the change is scoped to the `message` tool only and merely lets the *existing* critical/no-progress thresholds apply as designed; no threshold, cap, or other detector/tool is altered.

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Agents / tools

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #89090

prs.title: fix(gateway): restart cleanly on bundled dist module rotation after in-place upgrade
prs.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: Issue #88857 reports that the gateway crashes with an unhandled `SyntaxError` claiming `./provider-discovery.runtime.js` does not export `n` — even though the export is present in the bundled file — after an in-place package upgrade (`npm i -g openclaw@latest` via the updater) while the process keeps running. It surfaces as a mysterious crash and writes an `unhandled_rejection` stability bundle.
- **Root Cause**: The lazy runtime boundaries are emitted on **stable filenames** (`*.runtime.js`, see `tsdown.config.ts`) but their minified **export names** vary per build. After an in-place upgrade, a content-hashed chunk already loaded in the running process lazily imports a stable-named `*.runtime.js` whose rebuilt copy on disk now exports a differently minified name, so Node raises `SyntaxError: ... does not provide an export named 'n'` (or `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` for a rotated chunk). The module graph is broken and the rejection falls through `installUnhandledRejectionHandler` to the default path, which logs it as an unhandled rejection and writes a crash stability bundle. A build-level "stable export names" change would only help *future* upgrades — where both the running and replacing builds already carry stable names — and only for the export-mismatch branch, not `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` and not the build already in memory expecting the old name. Because the importing chunk is already loaded, the only correct response to "old process + replaced files" is a clean restart onto the consistent new build, so a runtime safety net is needed regardless of any build-side hardening.
- **Fix**: Recognize this dist module-graph rotation in the unhandled-rejection handler and restart cleanly instead of crashing.
  - `isDistModuleRotationError(err)` classifies a `SyntaxError` with `does not provide an export named` (or an `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND`) whose message names a `*runtime.js` boundary, walking the cause chain via the existing `collectNestedUnhandledErrorCandidates`. It is scoped strictly to OpenClaw's own bundled dist: `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` carries the absolute `node_modules/openclaw/dist/…runtime.js` path in its message and is matched there; the export-mismatch `SyntaxError` names only the relative import specifier (which carries no ownership — and our boundary set includes bare and hyphen `runtime.js` names too), so it additionally requires the importing frame in the **stack** to be under `openclaw/dist`. A dependency/plugin's own `dist/runtime.js` failure keeps the normal fatal path.
  - The handler logs it as the expected in-place-upgrade event, restores the terminal, and exits for a clean supervisor restart onto the consistent new build — but **skips the fatal-error hooks** (the only registered one writes a crash stability bundle), so an expected upgrade restart no longer crashes with an unhandled rejection or leaves a crash bundle behind.
  - The failing boundary (and any absolute path) is read from the **message**; for the export-mismatch case the importing `openclaw/dist` frame is read from the **stack** purely as a positive ownership gate (AND-narrowing), never as a trigger — so a third-party `dist/` path in a stack frame can never by itself cause a match.
- **What changed**:
  - `src/infra/unhandled-rejections.ts` — add the `isDistModuleRotationError` classifier and branch it ahead of the default unhandled-rejection exit.
  - `src/infra/unhandled-rejections.test.ts` — classifier coverage (positive + scoping negatives).
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - The bundler/build config (`tsdown.config.ts`) is untouched — ruled out as ineffective for the already-running process and risky for bundle size/determinism.
  - The existing classifiers, the fatal/config/transient branches, and the uncaught-exception path are unchanged.
  - No config surface (no schema, defaults, doctor migrations, or `docs/reference/config`).
  - No plugin surface (no plugin SDK, manifest, `extensions/*` api/runtime-api, registry/loader).
  - Gateway protocol unchanged.
  - The updater's own upgrade sequence is unchanged. The managed `openclaw update` path already stops the gateway service before replacing the package (`maybeStopManagedServiceBeforePackageUpdate` in `update-command.ts`), so it does not hit this. This is the safety net for the case the managed path does not cover: a manual/direct `npm i -g openclaw@latest` (or any out-of-band `dist/` replacement) under a still-running gateway.

### Reproduction

1. Run a long-lived gateway from a global install (`npm i -g openclaw`).
2. Upgrade in place under the running process (`npm i -g openclaw@latest`, as the updater does), rotating `dist/` chunk hashes while the old chunks stay in memory.
3. A path that lazily imports a `*.runtime.js` boundary runs after the upgrade.
4. **Before this PR**: Node raises `SyntaxError: ... does not provide an export named 'n'`; it falls through to the default handler and is logged as `[openclaw] Unhandled promise rejection`, writing an `unhandled_rejection` stability bundle — looks like a mysterious crash.
5. **After this PR**: the handler classifies it as a dist module rotation, logs `Bundled dist changed under the running gateway (in-place upgrade); restarting to load the new build`, and exits cleanly for a supervisor restart onto the consistent new build — without an unhandled-rejection crash and without writing a crash stability bundle.

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed (#88857)**: the in-place-upgrade export-name mismatch is no longer a mysterious unhandled-rejection crash that writes a stability bundle; it is classified and exits as an expected, clearly-logged restart that reloads the new build, and the crash stability bundle is not written for it.

**Real environment tested (Linux, Node 22 — Vitest against the production classifier and the installed unhandled-rejection handler)**: the classifier is exercised directly with the reporter's exact `SyntaxError`, `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND`, a cause-chain wrapper, and scoping negatives; the handler is driven end-to-end via `process.emit("unhandledRejection", ...)` with `process.exit` / `restoreTerminalState` mocked to assert the restart path skips the fatal hooks.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `pnpm test src/infra/unhandled-rejections.test.ts src/infra/unhandled-rejections.fatal-detection.test.ts`; `pnpm tsgo`; `pnpm exec oxfmt --check` and `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` on the changed files.

**Evidence after fix (Vitest output for the touched test files)**:

```text
 unhandled-rejections.test.ts                  Tests  74 passed (74)
 unhandled-rejections.fatal-detection.test.ts  Tests  10 passed (10)
```

The classifier gains nine cases: positives (export-name mismatch from a rotated dist runtime boundary #88857, rotated-chunk `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND`, cause-chain wrapper, and OpenClaw's own `node_modules/openclaw/dist` boundary) and scoping negatives (genuine source `SyntaxError`, an export-mismatch outside the bundled dist, a bare third-party `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND`, and a third-party package's own `node_modules/<pkg>/dist/runtime.js` for both `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` and export-mismatch). The handler gains two: a dist-rotation rejection exits `[1]`, restores the terminal with reason `dist module rotation`, and does **not** invoke the fatal hooks (so no crash stability bundle); an unrelated rejection still exits `[1]` and **does** invoke the fatal hooks.

**Observed result after fix**: a dist runtime-boundary rotation error is classified true and routed to a clean restart that skips the fatal hooks (no crash stability bundle); unrelated SyntaxErrors, non-runtime export mismatches, and third-party missing modules classify false and keep their existing fatal/crash behavior.

**What was not tested**: a live in-place `npm i -g` upgrade under a running launchd gateway on the reporter's macOS host. The classification and the handler restart path (including skipping the fatal hooks) are covered deterministically against the production function and the installed handler; a full upgrade-under-load round-trip was not driven.

**Repro confirmation**: each new case fails on a pristine tree (`isDistModuleRotationError is not a function`) and passes with the fix, so the tests cover the production change.

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: Misclassifying an unrelated error as a benign restart could mask a real crash. **Mitigation**: the match requires a specific signature (export-name mismatch `SyntaxError` or `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND`), a `*runtime.js` boundary named in the message, AND positive OpenClaw-own-dist ownership — the absolute `openclaw/dist` path in the message for `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND`, or the importing `openclaw/dist` stack frame for the relative-specifier export mismatch — AND that the named path is not another package's `node_modules/<pkg>/dist`. So a dependency/plugin module-load failure keeps the normal fatal path. Negatives (third-party `dist/runtime.js`, plus third-party bare- and relative-specifier `*runtime.js` export mismatches whose importing frame is not OpenClaw's) are covered by tests.
- **Risk**: A genuine packaging regression — a release that truly ships a `*runtime.js` missing an export — hits the same signature and is reframed from a loud `unhandled_rejection` crash (with a stability bundle) into a quieter "in-place upgrade restart" with no crash bundle, which can make a real build defect look benign. **Mitigation**: it does not introduce a new crash loop (the prior default path already exited and the supervisor already restarted into the same broken build); the distinct `console.error` line is still emitted on every occurrence, so a repeating one is greppable in the gateway log even without a bundle. This is an accepted trade for the common in-place-upgrade case, called out here explicitly; if maintainers prefer a lightweight record over none, a non-crash diagnostic line could be added on this path.
- **Risk**: Consulting the stack could let an unrelated third-party `dist/` stack frame trigger a benign restart. **Mitigation**: the failing boundary is always named in the **message** (ESM export-mismatch and `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` both name the specifier/module there), which is what triggers a candidate match; the stack is read only to *confirm* OpenClaw-own-dist ownership for the relative-specifier case, as a positive AND-gate, so a third-party stack frame can only ever narrow — never by itself cause — a match.

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] CI/CD / infra

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #88857

prs.title: Keep agent web_search on runtime provider resolution
prs.body:
Fixes #87347

## User-facing bug
Agent-side `web_search` could report `web_search is disabled or no provider is available` even when the Brave web search provider was already loaded in the runtime and worked through `openclaw infer web`. The agent tool was deciding that a manifest-owned configured provider should avoid runtime provider discovery before `runWebSearch` had a chance to use the active runtime registry.

## Exact repro
Source-level reproduction:

1. Configure `tools.web.search.provider` to a manifest-owned provider such as `brave`.
2. Start a runtime where the Brave web search provider is loaded and visible through runtime/plugin inspection.
3. Invoke the agent-side `web_search` tool with no active runtime metadata on the tool call.
4. Before this fix, `resolveWebSearchToolRuntimeContext()` consulted manifest ownership and passed `preferRuntimeProviders: false` to `runWebSearch`, so execution could diverge from the loaded runtime provider view.
5. After this fix, agent-side `web_search` keeps `preferRuntimeProviders: true`; `runWebSearch` still applies manifest ownership as a runtime load-scope hint, but it loads from the live runtime registry first.

## Fix summary
- Kept agent-side `web_search` runtime-provider discovery enabled regardless of configured provider manifest ownership.
- Left `web_fetch` behavior unchanged: bundled fetch owners still disable runtime provider discovery where that path expects static/bundled provider loading.
- Updated the late-bind and runtime-context tests so manifest-owned configured search providers now prove the runtime-first path instead of the old static-provider preference.

## Targeted tests
- Updated `src/agents/tools/web-tool-runtime-context.test.ts` to assert search never resolves manifest owners at the agent-tool preference gate, while fetch still does.
- Updated `src/agents/tools/web-search.late-bind.test.ts` to assert configured manifest-owned search providers pass `preferRuntimeProviders: true` into `runWebSearch`.
- Re-ran the existing `web_tools` defaults tests and focused `web-search/runtime` provider-loading tests to cover the downstream runtime behavior.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Agent-side `web_search` now uses the same live runtime provider path as `runWebSearch` / `openclaw infer web`, instead of switching configured manifest-owned providers to static provider loading at the agent-tool layer.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/agent-web-search-provider-resolution`, commit `1c171b7e`, rebased onto `origin/main` at `be127318`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/agents/tools/web-tool-runtime-context.ts src/agents/tools/web-tool-runtime-context.test.ts src/agents/tools/web-search.late-bind.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --run --maxWorkers 1 src/agents/tools/web-tool-runtime-context.test.ts src/agents/tools/web-search.late-bind.test.ts src/agents/tools/web-tools.enabled-defaults.test.ts src/web-search/runtime.test.ts -t "web tool runtime context|web_search late-bound runtime fallback|web tools defaults|scopes runtime provider loading|honors preferRuntimeProviders|keeps runtime provider loading"`
- `codex review --base origin/main`

Evidence after fix:
- Targeted Vitest passed: `Test Files 7 passed (7)`, `Tests 40 passed | 23 skipped (63)`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Copied terminal output from the local provider-resolution proof shows the agent tool keeping runtime provider discovery instead of resolving a static bundled provider:

```text
web_search tool context:
  provider source: runtime
  preferRuntimeProviders: true
  configured provider: brave
  loaded provider count before static fallback: 1
  selected execution path: runWebSearch runtime provider
```

- `codex review --base origin/main` passed with no actionable regressions.

Observed result after fix: Manifest-owned configured search providers keep runtime discovery enabled at the agent tool boundary; the runtime path remains responsible for provider scoping.

What was not tested: `pnpm check:changed` did not reach changed-file checks. In the sandbox it failed during `pnpm install` with npm registry DNS `ENOTFOUND`. An escalated retry got past DNS but still failed during `pnpm install` when Darwin optional package downloads timed out with error `23`.

## Not tested / known gaps
- No live Brave API request was run from this environment; validation is source-level with targeted runtime/tool tests.
- No live Brave-backed web search request was run from a real agent conversation; coverage is through provider-resolution tests.

## AI-assisted disclosure
This patch was prepared with AI assistance. I used source-level reproduction, targeted regression tests, and `codex review --base origin/main` to validate the behavior above.

prs.title: Tag embedded subagent gap-fill rows as delivery mirrors
prs.body:
Fixes #87329

## User-facing bug
Subagent announce / embedded handoff gap-fill can append a user-visible assistant mirror using the child runtime provider and model metadata, such as `anthropic/claude-*`, instead of marking the row as an OpenClaw transcript-only delivery mirror. Replay filters only exclude `provider: "openclaw"` with `model: "delivery-mirror"` / `gateway-injected`, so a display-only mirror can later be replayed back into a provider turn. In conversations with provider-managed thinking/signature blocks, that stale mirror can surface as errors like "thinking blocks cannot be modified."

## Exact repro
Source-level reproduction:

1. Run `persistCliTurnTranscript` for an embedded assistant gap-fill with:
   - `embeddedAssistantGapFill: true`
   - `result.meta.agentMeta.provider: "claude-cli"` or another runtime provider
   - visible final text in `result.meta.finalAssistantVisibleText`
2. Before this fix, the appended transcript assistant row used `api: "cli"`, `provider: "claude-cli"`, and `model: "opus"`.
3. That row is user-visible display state, not provider conversation state, so replay filters do not identify it as transcript-only.
4. After this fix, the same gap-fill row is written as `api: "openai-responses"`, `provider: "openclaw"`, and `model: "delivery-mirror"`.

## Fix summary
- Changed embedded assistant gap-fill transcript persistence to write assistant mirrors as `openclaw/delivery-mirror`.
- Kept normal CLI transcript persistence unchanged: non-gap-fill CLI replies still preserve real CLI provider/model metadata and usage.
- Added regression coverage so the gap-fill path now asserts the transcript-only mirror metadata.

## Targeted tests
- Extended `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts` to prove embedded gap-fill rows are tagged as `openclaw/delivery-mirror`.
- Re-ran replay-history coverage to confirm `delivery-mirror` rows remain excluded from embedded provider replay.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Embedded subagent assistant gap-fill rows are now stored as OpenClaw delivery mirrors, so provider replay filters do not treat display-only text as provider conversation state.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/subagent-announce-delivery-metadata`, commit `3ba0619f`, rebased onto `origin/main` at `be127318`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --run --maxWorkers 1 src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`
- `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --run --maxWorkers 1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/replay-history.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`
- `codex review --base origin/main`

Evidence after fix:
- Command execution Vitest passed: `Test Files 2 passed (2)`, `Tests 62 passed (62)`.
- Replay-history plus command execution Vitest passed: `Test Files 3 passed (3)`, `Tests 87 passed (87)`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Downstream compatibility search after review feedback: `rg -n "api:\\s*['\"]cli['\"]|api === ['\"]cli['\"]|api !== ['\"]cli['\"]|provider:\\s*['\"]openclaw['\"]|delivery-mirror|gateway-injected|gap-fill|embeddedAssistantGapFill|finalAssistantVisibleText" src ui packages extensions test docs -g '!node_modules'` found replay/debug-sensitive filtering keyed on `provider: "openclaw"` plus `model: "delivery-mirror"` / `"gateway-injected"` in `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/replay-history.ts` and `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts`, not on the old gap-fill row `api: "cli"` shape. The one user-visible compatibility surface is metadata display: `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts` can show a non-`gateway-injected` model label, so gap-fill rows may now surface `delivery-mirror` metadata instead of the child runtime model.
- `codex review --base origin/main` passed with no actionable regressions found.

Observed result after fix: Embedded gap-fill transcript rows use `api: "openai-responses"`, `provider: "openclaw"`, and `model: "delivery-mirror"` while normal CLI transcript rows still preserve real provider/model metadata.

What was not tested: `pnpm check:changed` did not reach changed-file checks because dependency installation hit npm registry DNS failures (`ENOTFOUND`) in the sandbox.

## Not tested / known gaps
- No live provider-managed thinking/signature conversation was replayed end to end; coverage is through transcript persistence and replay-history tests.
- No external transcript/debug consumer was found in this checkout that depends on `api: "cli"` for embedded gap-fill rows, but external scripts that read raw transcript metadata may observe the intentional gap-fill metadata change from child CLI provider/model to `openclaw/delivery-mirror`.

## AI-assisted disclosure
This patch was prepared with AI assistance. I used local code inspection, targeted regression tests, and explicit command output to validate the behavior above.

prs.body:
## Summary

- filter provider/runtime-plan normalized tools through runtime-compatible schema inspection before metadata preservation
- preserve plugin/channel metadata on normalized descriptors before quarantine diagnostics are emitted
- make runtime tool metadata preservation tolerate unreadable normalized `name` descriptors while keeping the healthy-path array identity contract

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/runtime-plan/tools.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/agents/runtime-plan/tools.ts src/agents/runtime-plan/tools.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: provider/runtime-plan tool normalization can no longer return an unreadable or runtime-incompatible normalized descriptor that crashes metadata preservation before the assistant turn can continue.

Real environment tested: local Codex linked worktree for focused Vitest, formatting, lint, diff check, and branch autoreview; AWS Crabbox Linux for changed-gate proof.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the focused Vitest command above, touched-file `oxfmt`/`oxlint`, `git diff --check`, branch autoreview, and AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed`.

Evidence after fix: focused Vitest passed for `src/agents/runtime-plan/tools.test.ts`; branch autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings with `overall: patch is correct (0.86)`; AWS Crabbox run `run_7865bb3dc188` on lease `cbx_1d123e4cc6da` passed lanes `core, coreTests` with exit 0 and stopped the lease.

Observed result after fix: poisoned normalized tool descriptors are quarantined through the runtime-compatible projection path, diagnostics receive metadata-preserved normalized tools for owner attribution, and surviving healthy normalized tools keep copied plugin metadata.

What was not tested: full suite, Docker, live provider, and E2E lanes. Blacksmith Testbox was unavailable in this shell because `blacksmith auth status` reported no authenticated organization, so the broad changed proof used AWS Crabbox instead.

prs.title: Preserve Telegram streamed text blocks between tool calls
prs.body:
Fixes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87326.

## Summary

- Seal Telegram `info.kind === "block"` draft previews as finalized visible messages instead of leaving them as mutable previews.
- Reuse the existing assistant-message-start rotation path so later assistant blocks and the final answer use a new Telegram message.
- Keep text-only tool progress transient by scoping finalization to answer blocks, not `info.kind === "tool"`.

## User-facing bug

When Telegram block streaming was enabled, answer text emitted between tool calls could be visible briefly and then disappear. The next assistant block or final answer edited the same draft preview, so users could lose intermediate findings such as "Site A shows X" after the agent checked another tool.

## Exact repro

The focused regression drives Telegram dispatch through the same sequence as the issue: answer block, assistant-message/tool-call boundary, second answer block, another boundary, final answer.

```bash
CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --run --maxWorkers 1 extensions/telegram/src/lane-delivery.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts
```

## Fix summary

`createLaneTextDeliverer` now treats streamed answer blocks as finalizable previews. It stops the active draft stream, marks the lane finalized, and returns `preview-finalized`; then `onAssistantMessageStart` can rotate to a fresh Telegram message before the next assistant block. Telegram dispatch now mirrors any finalized answer preview, not only final-answer previews, so retained block messages follow the same outbound hook/prompt-context path as streamed finals.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Telegram answer blocks between tool calls remain as separate visible draft messages instead of being overwritten by later blocks.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on Node v24.4.1, Telegram dispatch tests with mocked Telegram API/draft stream, commit `52f1b822`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
git diff --check
```

```bash
CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --run --maxWorkers 1 extensions/telegram/src/lane-delivery.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts
```

```bash
CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --run --maxWorkers 1 extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/lane-delivery.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix: `bot-message-dispatch.test.ts` now asserts the sequence `Site A shows X.`, `Site B shows Y. Now checking site C.`, and `Done. A, B, and C were checked.` is delivered through three draft updates with two `forceNewMessage()` rotations. Copied terminal output from the local Telegram dispatch proof:

```text
draft update #1: Site A shows X.
forceNewMessage()
draft update #2: Site B shows Y. Now checking site C.
forceNewMessage()
draft update #3: Done. A, B, and C were checked.
```

Observed result after fix: `lane-delivery.test.ts` + `bot-message-dispatch.test.ts` passed, 125 tests. `draft-stream.test.ts` + `lane-delivery.test.ts` passed, 69 tests. `git diff --check` passed.

What was not tested: Live Telegram bot roundtrip was not run because no Telegram token/chat credentials are configured in this workspace. `pnpm check:changed` is blocked because `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"` fails crabbox binary sanity checks. `tsgo` is blocked because `node_modules/.bin/tsgo` is still missing after both sandboxed and escalated `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` attempts timed out while fetching npm packages. `codex review --base origin/main` is blocked because the sandboxed run cannot initialize the local app-server client and the escalated run was rejected by the Codex usage limit until 18:34.

## AI-assisted disclosure

This change was implemented with AI assistance. `codex review --base origin/main` could not complete locally for the reasons listed above.

prs.title: feat(subagents): forward toolsAllow from sessions_spawn
prs.body:
## Summary
- Add optional `toolsAllow` to `sessions_spawn` for native embedded subagent runs.
- Validate it as a string array, preserve explicit `[]`, and reject unsupported ACP or CLI-backed native paths instead of silently ignoring the policy.
- Forward and persist the allowlist through Gateway/session/embedded-attempt surfaces so later native child turns inherit the stored tool policy.
- Update protocol/docs/tests/prompt snapshots for the additive field. No `CHANGELOG.md` change is kept.

## Current upstream status
Latest `origin/main` already has broad May subagent/runtime work and existing embedded-runner `toolsAllow` support, but `sessions_spawn` still does not expose, forward, or persist a per-spawn `toolsAllow` entry point. This PR is narrowed to that missing public/control-plane plumbing.

## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Feature

## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Documentation

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related #78381
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can constrain native embedded subagent tools with `toolsAllow`; the same policy is persisted on child sessions and inherited by later embedded child turns when no explicit override is passed.

Real environment tested: macOS 26.2, Node v25.9.0, pnpm 11.2.2, local OpenClaw source checkout, current PR head `1bda9a56c450bbdb03b7fbbcd670b5d7f5cf1bac`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `openclaw.json`, `pnpm openclaw agent --local`, and repo mock OpenAI `/v1/responses` server with request logging.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Seeded a temp `sessions.json` child session at `agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-1bda9a56c4` with `runtimeToolsAllow: ["read", "exec"]`, started `scripts/e2e/mock-openai-server.mjs` with `MOCK_REQUEST_LOG`, then ran `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof.hSSPqV/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof.hSSPqV/state/openclaw.json OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-openclaw-78441-proof pnpm openclaw agent --local --session-key agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-1bda9a56c4 --message "Reply with exactly OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441" --json`, followed by a Node assertion over the mock request log.

Evidence after fix: Copied live output from the request-log assertion:

```json
{
  "head": "1bda9a56c450bbdb03b7fbbcd670b5d7f5cf1bac",
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-1bda9a56c4",
  "responsesCalls": 1,
  "inheritedToolNames": [
    "exec",
    "read"
  ],
  "markerObserved": true
}
```

Observed result after fix: The later CLI turn succeeded, reached the mock `/v1/responses` endpoint exactly once, returned `OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441`, and the provider request exposed only the inherited `exec` and `read` tools from the persisted child-session `runtimeToolsAllow` field.

What was not tested: This proof used a mock OpenAI server instead of live OpenAI credentials and did not start a long-running Gateway process. Focused tests cover the `sessions_spawn` writer/forwarding path; this proof verifies the real later-turn CLI/provider request behavior.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] Prompt snapshot check
- Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`, `src/commands/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`, `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*`
- Scenario the tests lock in: `toolsAllow` is exposed, normalized, validates string arrays, preserves explicit `[]`, rejects unsupported runtimes, forwards through Gateway/session state, reaches embedded runs started by the command runner, and appears in model-facing dynamic tool snapshots.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
`runtime="subagent"` callers may pass `toolsAllow` to narrow a spawned native embedded subagent's tool set. Defaults are unchanged when omitted. `toolsAllow: []` intentionally runs the child without tools. Unsupported ACP and CLI-backed native paths reject the option clearly.

## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: this only narrows the child tool set for native embedded subagent runs. Unsupported paths reject the option so callers do not get a false sense that an allowlist was enforced.

## Repro + Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed: 5 files, 159 tests, 3 Vitest shards.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo` passed.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ...changed files...` passed.
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check` passed: `Prompt snapshots are current (7 files)`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts --reporter=dot src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` passed locally after CI showed a timeout/interrupt flake in that unrelated file: 1 file, 48 tests.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check` passed.
- `pnpm build` passed before the snapshot commit; `pnpm openclaw agent --help` rebuilt dist successfully after the snapshot commit because the CLI detected a new git head.
- Current-head CLI/mock OpenAI proof above passed.

## Human Verification (required)
- Verified scenarios: schema exposure, native forwarding path, Gateway forwarding path, session persistence, embedded command runner propagation, persisted child-session allowlist inheritance, strict validation tests, ACP rejection tests, CLI-backed native fail-closed behavior for non-empty and empty allowlists, embedded empty-allowlist behavior, prompt snapshot update, formatter/typecheck/build/whitespace checks.
- Edge cases checked: omitted `toolsAllow`, explicit empty `[]`, blank strings trimmed/filtered, non-string entries rejected, non-array input rejected, ACP runtime rejected, CLI-backed native runtime rejected.
- What you did **not** verify: a live provider-backed spawned child run with real OpenAI credentials.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect ACP runtime spawns.
  - Mitigation: the tool rejects `toolsAllow` for `runtime="acp"` with a clear input error.
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect CLI-backed native runs.
  - Mitigation: CLI-backed native runs reject `toolsAllow` before dispatch because they cannot enforce the embedded-runner allowlist.
- Risk: public API naming/semantics need maintainer judgment.
  - Mitigation: the implementation is additive and reuses the existing embedded-runner allowlist semantics rather than adding a second filtering mechanism.

prs.title: Render dashboard chat history incrementally
prs.body:
Fixes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/87345

## User-facing bug
Switching dashboard chat sessions could synchronously rebuild the full 100-message UI history window, causing 8-10s switches on long or heavy transcripts.

## Exact repro
1. Open the Control UI chat dashboard.
2. Use or select a session whose `chat.history` returns the current 100-message tail.
3. Switch between dashboard sessions.
4. Before: the chat view can block while all loaded history items are built and rendered at once.
5. After: newly loaded large histories start with the latest 30-message render window, expand in 30-message batches when the user is at the top, avoid programmatic bottom-scroll expansion, and auto-fill non-scrollable initial windows while preserving bottom anchoring.

## Fix summary
- Added optional `historyRenderLimit` to `buildChatItems`, preserving existing default 100-message behavior.
- Added per-session render-window state in `renderChat` so newly loaded large histories initially render 30 messages.
- Expand the render window on top scroll or exact top state, skip bottom auto-scrolls inside the top threshold, and auto-fill after render when the initial window does not overflow.
- Keep auto-filled windows anchored to latest messages via the existing `onScrollToBottom` path.
- Search with a non-empty query still renders the full loaded and capped history window.

## Targeted tests

- `buildChatItems` coverage proves the render limit preserves the newest window.
- Chat view tests cover initial 30-message windows, top-scroll expansion, bottom-scroll suppression, auto-fill, and full rendering while searching.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Switching to a large dashboard chat history no longer forces an immediate 100-message render; the UI starts with a latest-message window and expands incrementally.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/dashboard-session-switching`, commit `16fc1330`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --write --threads=1 ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.ts ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --run --maxWorkers 1 ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts -t "chat history render window"` (6 passed | 71 skipped)
- `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --run --maxWorkers 1 ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts` (95 passed)
- `codex review --base origin/main` passed after resolving review findings.

Evidence after fix:
- Focused render-window Vitest passed after adding the repeated top-scroll anchor regression from review feedback. Copied terminal output:

```text
RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/alex/PR/projects/openclaw__openclaw/worktrees/88685

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  6 passed | 71 skipped (77)
```

- Broader UI chat Vitest passed: 95 passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- `codex review --base origin/main` passed after resolving review findings.

Observed result after fix: Newly loaded large histories render the newest 30 messages first, expand in 30-message batches from top-scroll/top state, preserve the previously visible top anchor across repeated expansion, avoid bottom auto-scroll expansion, and still render full loaded history for non-empty search.

What was not tested: `pnpm tsgo:test:ui` and `node scripts/check-changed.mjs` could not complete because both invoke `pnpm install`; sandbox got npm `ENOTFOUND`, and the escalated retry hit Darwin optional package download `error (23) The operation was aborted due to timeout`. Direct `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.ui.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-ui.tsbuildinfo` could not run because `node_modules/.bin/tsgo` was missing after the blocked install. No browser screenshot or video was captured; this PR changes render-window behavior and is covered with jsdom view tests, not visual layout changes. A full local rerun of `ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts` in the 88685 worktree still has three unrelated existing composer/Talk assertion failures, while the focused `chat history render window` target passes.

## Not tested / known gaps
- `pnpm tsgo:test:ui` and `node scripts/check-changed.mjs` could not complete because both invoke `pnpm install`; sandbox got npm `ENOTFOUND`, and the escalated retry hit Darwin optional package download `error (23) The operation was aborted due to timeout`.
- Direct `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.ui.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-ui.tsbuildinfo` could not run because `node_modules/.bin/tsgo` was missing after the blocked install.
- Full local `ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts` rerun in the 88685 worktree still has three unrelated composer/Talk assertion failures; the focused render-window target added here passes.
- No browser screenshot or video captured; this PR changes render-window behavior and is covered with jsdom view tests, not visual layout changes.

## AI-assisted disclosure
Prepared with AI assistance in Codex. I reviewed the diff, ran targeted tests, and resolved codex review findings before opening.
